scenarios.description: parse(File,charsetName) loads file with explicit charset and resolves baseUri from file path
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists with known content
scenarios.expectedResult: Document contains parsed content and baseUri equals file absolute path
pseudocode: TEST TC12 parse two-arg File overload

scenarios.description: parse(File) loads file with default charset detection and resolves baseUri from file path
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists with known content
scenarios.expectedResult: Document contains parsed content and baseUri equals file absolute path
pseudocode: TEST TC13 parse no-arg File overload

scenarios.description: parse(File,charsetName,baseUri,Parser) uses provided parser for file content
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists, xml parser provided
scenarios.expectedResult: Document parsed by xmlParser yields expected tag structure
pseudocode: TEST TC14 parse four-arg File overload with XML parser
Document doc = Jsoup.parse(file, charset, baseUri, parser);

scenarios.description: parse(InputStream,charsetName,baseUri,Parser) parses stream with HTML parser overload
scenarios.precondition: InputStream of HTML fragment, htmlParser provided
scenarios.expectedResult: Document parsed by htmlParser contains expected element
pseudocode: TEST TC15 parse four-arg InputStream overload with HTML parser
Document doc = Jsoup.parse(in, charset, baseUri, parser);

scenarios.description: parse(Path,charsetName,baseUri) loads file via Path with explicit charset
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists at Path with known content
scenarios.expectedResult: Document contains parsed content and baseUri equals provided baseUri
pseudocode: TEST TC16 parse three-arg Path overload

scenarios.description: parse(Path,charsetName) loads file via Path with explicit charset and derives baseUri from path
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists at Path
scenarios.expectedResult: Document contains parsed content and baseUri equals absolute path string
pseudocode: TEST TC17 parse two-arg Path overload

scenarios.description: parse(Path) loads file via Path with default charset and derives baseUri from path
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists at Path
scenarios.expectedResult: Document contains parsed content and baseUri equals absolute path string
pseudocode: TEST TC18 parse no-arg Path overload

scenarios.description: parse(Path,charsetName,baseUri,Parser) uses XML parser for Path-based input
scenarios.precondition: temp HTML file exists at Path, xmlParser provided
scenarios.expectedResult: Document parsed by xmlParser yields expected self-closed element
pseudocode: TEST TC19 parse four-arg Path overload with XML parser
Document doc = Jsoup.parse(path, charset, baseUri, parser);

scenarios.description: parse((String)null) throws NullPointerException when html is null in single-arg overload
parse(URL,timeoutMillis) successfully fetches and parses an HTTP URL with stub server
scenarios.precondition: local HTTP server stub returns 200 with known HTML
scenarios.expectedResult: Document contains parsed element from stub response
pseudocode: TEST TC21 parse URL overload success
